ideation: This animation sequence depicts the act of kicking a scooter into motion. It focuses on the natural arm movements involved in maintaining balance and propelling oneself forward.  The left arm will act as a counterbalance, while the right arm will initiate the kicking motion. The lack of elbows in the robot's design necessitates creative use of shoulder joints to convey the dynamic movement. Frame 1 will show the robot in a poised position, ready to start kicking. The right arm will be slightly bent and back as if preparing to push off the ground and the left arm will be in a natural counterbalance position. Frame 2 will show the right arm pushing backward, generating power for the kick. The left arm will swing forward slightly for better balance. Frame 3 will show the right arm returning to a neutral position as the robot starts moving forward, while the left arm extends outward to maintain balance. Frame 4 will show the robot in motion, arms slightly forward to maintain balance and stability.

frames.beat: arms poised, right arm back, ready to push
right arm powerfully kicks back, left arm balances forward
right arm returns, left arm extends for stability
robot glides, arms forward for balance
